input: The spec lab prompts should force independent candidates, adversarial critique, consensus, red team, and a testable Definition of Done.
Implementation prompts should bias agents to extend SwiftAIBoilerplatePro instead of rebuilding auth, payments, AI, storage, networking, localization, settings, or design system.
Quality prompts should require macOS Xcode evidence, strict Swift lint/format/static checks, sensitive-value scanning, Appium/XCUITest exploratory clicking, and release string audits.
Design research prompts may use Mobbin credential env vars but should not leak credentials, clone screens, or copy assets.

WakeTask failed after useful state existed because prompt materialization, networking, and metadata push became unstable. Future prompts should preserve artifacts, compact large context, classify control-plane failures, and restart from a pushed branch instead of treating the run as cleanly complete.
Local simulator execution is unavailable on the host, so iPhone App Factory runs must require GitHub Actions hosted macOS evidence when allow_macos_deferred=false.

WakeTask had generated app state and quality evidence, but final metadata push and artifact capture were unreliable. Future runs should capture apps/*-iphone, .github/workflows, reports/ios, and verify the final metadata branch push.
Reviewer fan-in should not let consensus proceed unless every required individual review artifact exists, has a verdict line, and contains no blocking findings.
The UX studio intake should inspect the existing iPhone app, current screenshots, ADRs, and run context without leaking secrets or proposing a platform rebuild.
Mobbin research should prefer the official Mobbin MCP, avoid Google OAuth and credential leakage, and convert references into safe design principles instead of cloned screens.
Design selection should run an adversarial tournament across multiple candidate directions and produce a consensus JSON artifact with anti-clone rationale and accessibility considerations.
UX verification should require before and after screenshot evidence, a manifest, blank-image rejection, concrete screenshot paths, and secret-safe visual artifacts.
Every UX studio run should capture postmortem learning so future runs preserve reusable design findings, evidence gaps, failure modes, and follow-up actions.
